# Observable Plot

## Source Record
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| URL | `https://observablehq.com/plot/` |
| Owner | Observable |
| Scope | Plot homepage covering the library model, feature areas, example gallery, and docs entrypoints |
| Why it matters to nbadb | Observable Plot is a strong candidate for lightweight, code-first statistical and basketball-specific charts in docs or downstream apps |

## Summary
Observable Plot positions itself as a concise JavaScript library for exploratory data visualization. The homepage emphasizes a grammar built from layered marks rather than fixed chart types, plus first-class scales, transforms, facets, and geographic projections.

## Key Points
- Plot frames charts as compositions of marks such as bars, dots, and lines instead of preset chart widgets.
- The library highlights scales, transforms, facets, and projections as core primitives.
- The docs homepage links directly to getting started, conceptual overview, and a gallery of examples.
- The example gallery includes a basketball shot chart, which is directly relevant to NBA-oriented presentation work.
- Plot is presented as D3-adjacent and built by the Observable team.

## nbadb Relevance
- A good fit for docs-site visual examples where concise declarative chart code matters.
- The mark-and-transform model maps well to warehouse-shaped outputs and aggregation tables.
- The basketball shot chart example makes it especially relevant for spatial NBA visualizations.
- Could be a lighter-weight alternative to more app-centric chart libraries for editorial or exploratory pages.
